title: NOC 73202 – Pest controllers and fumigators | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73202 professionals are the specialized frontline of public health and structural protection in Canada. As a TEER 3 occupation, these experts inspect properties for infestations and apply chemical or mechanical treatments to eliminate pests, rodents, and destructive insects. In 2026, as Canada faces shifting climate patterns that alter pest migration and increases focus on "Integrated Pest Management" (IPM), these workers are classified as Essential Health and Safety Talent. For immigration, this role is a key target for the 2026 Express Entry Trade Category and the Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level3Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group732 – Building maintenance installers, servicers and repairersMinor Group7320 – Building maintenance installers, servicers and repairersNOC Code73202

duties: Site Inspection: Inspect buildings and surrounding outdoor areas at regular intervals to detect signs of infestation (e.g., droppings, damage, or nests).
Treatment Determination: Identify the specific type of pest and determine the most effective and safe treatment plan required.
Chemical Application: Prepare and spray chemical mixtures on infested areas using mechanical or electric sprayers to eliminate pests.
Household Fumigation: Execute controlled fumigation of residential or commercial structures using gas-based treatments for deep-seated infestations.
Wildlife Management: Place and set cage traps to safely capture and remove nuisance animals, such as raccoons, skunks, or squirrels.
Exclusion Services: Install physical animal control barriers such as needle strips, heavy-duty netting, and other hardware to prevent re-entry.
Sanitization: Clean out areas that harbor pests using specialized industrial tools like rakes, high-efficiency vacuums, or brooms.
Cost Estimation: Provide detailed on-site inspections and prepare formal cost estimates for treatments and long-term prevention plans.
Client Consultation: Advise residential and commercial clients on sanitary measures and structural repairs to prevent future infestations.
Regulatory Compliance: Maintain accurate records of chemical usage and site visits in compliance with provincial pesticide regulations and safety standards.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Training: Completion of specialized courses in pest control or extermination is mandatory, often followed by several months of on-the-job training.
Licensing (Critical): A Provincial Pesticide Applicator License is mandatory in all provinces to handle and apply regulated chemicals.
Security & Bonding: Most employers require a clean criminal record check (Bondable) as technicians frequently enter private homes and businesses.
Physical Stamina: The ability to work in confined spaces (attics/crawlspaces) and carry heavy equipment is essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, specialized Fumigation Leads or those in high-demand urban areas often earn annual salaries between $55,000 and $82,000, with significant bonuses for emergency or after-hours service calls.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that as Canada's urban density increases and climate change allows certain pest species to survive longer winters, the reliance on professional pest management will intensify. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), Retirements will be a primary driver of job openings, creating a steady stream of opportunities for internationally trained exterminators through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for pest controllers in 2026 is Stable to Good, with a "Good" outlook specifically in Ontario for the 2025–2027 period. In urban centers like Toronto and Vancouver, high-density housing and the expansion of the food service industry drive consistent demand. According to 2026 Job Bank data, the industry is seeing a moderate number of new positions due to employment growth and a high volume of upcoming retirements. Approximately 87% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time, which is higher than the national average for service-based trades.
Advanced Biosecurity Standards: Gain experience with the latest eco-friendly fumigants, biological controls, and digital heat-treatment technologies.
High Self-Employment Rate: This trade has a high rate of entrepreneurship (23% in Ontario), allowing skilled newcomers to eventually launch their own pest control businesses.
Recession-Resilient Industry: Pest control is an essential service for the hospitality, agriculture, and residential sectors, ensuring stable work regardless of economic shifts.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 3 role is a recognized trade for the 2026 Category-Based Selection in Express Entry, often offering a faster invitation path for skilled foreign workers.
pathways: Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): This NOC is a high priority for the 2026 category-based selection draws.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): For technicians with 2 years of experience and a valid job offer or certificate of qualification.
Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): A high-priority route for pest controllers with a valid job offer from an approved employer in the province.
Rural Community Immigration Pilot (RCIP): Ideal for those willing to work in regional hubs where pest management is critical for local agriculture and hospitality.
